description: Implement the backend-local relational GET seam needed for active relational GET-by-id and internal stored-document loads. Add `IRelationalGetRequest` carrying `MappingSet`, an unambiguous qualified-resource/base-resource identifier (do not rely on bare `ResourceName`), minimal readable-profile projection inputs (`ContentTypeDefinition` plus the precomputed top-level identity-property set), and an explicit local read mode such as `ExternalResponse` vs `StoredDocument`. Have Core `GetRequest` implement this contract internally and update both `GetByIdHandler` and `ProfileWriteValidationMiddleware` to populate it correctly. Keep DMS-990 scoped to GET-by-id: do not pull query-specific request plumbing forward unless a concrete live caller requires it. Acceptance: no public `Core.External` interface widening is required; relational GET can distinguish extension homographs and descriptor resources; internal stored-document fetches can suppress API metadata injection and readable projection without routing through a separate repository API.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering relational GET request construction, qualified-resource identification, read-mode selection, and readable-profile projection inputs.

description: Add explicit not-implemented read results and adjust the GET success contract for relational reads. Introduce `GetFailureNotImplemented` and `QueryFailureNotImplemented` (or equivalent) and map them to HTTP 501 in Core handlers. Relax `GetResult.GetSuccess.LastModifiedTraceId` to nullable/optional so relational GET can return stored last-modified metadata without inventing a trace id. Acceptance: unsupported relational query and descriptor GET no longer surface as `UnknownFailure`/HTTP 500; successful relational GET can return `null` trace id while preserving existing non-relational behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for `GetByIdHandler`, `QueryRequestHandler`, and relational repository stubs that assert 501/not-implemented behavior and nullable `LastModifiedTraceId` handling.

description: Fold descriptor URI projection into the same hydration batch used by relational reads, but keep this task scoped to batch SQL, result-shape contracts, and executor/reader changes. Extend `HydrationBatchBuilder`, `HydrationExecutor`, `HydrationReader`, and the hydration contracts so one DB command returns document metadata, hydrated table rows, and descriptor `(DescriptorId, Uri)` result sets in deterministic order for the selected keyset. Acceptance: GET-by-id hydration for relational-table resources uses one DB command/roundtrip for root, child, `_ext`, and descriptor URI data; descriptor result-set ordering is deterministic and matches compiled plan order; the batch contract is reusable by both external reads and current-state reconstitution consumers; DMS-992 still owns the dedicated descriptor behavior matrix; full provider-parity proof stays with the final GET-by-id integration tasks.
steps-to-verify: Add/extend unit tests for batch SQL ordering and executor result-set consumption, including descriptor result-set ordering.

category: Current-State Loader Uses Hydrated Descriptor Results
description: Migrate `RelationalWriteCurrentStateLoader` to consume descriptor URI data already present in the hydrated batch instead of issuing a second `DescriptorProjectionExecutor` command. Keep this task scoped to write-side current-state loading; external GET-by-id consumption of the same batched descriptor data belongs with repository orchestration. Acceptance: profile-driven stored-document reconstitution builds descriptor lookup state from hydrated result sets; relational-table current-state loads do not issue an extra descriptor SQL command; shared reconstitution behavior for write-side stored-document loading remains unchanged after the migration.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering current-state loading with batch-provided descriptor lookups, including a regression case for profile-driven stored-document reconstitution.

description: Close the remaining DMS-990 gaps in the existing shared JsonNode-based reconstitution engine rather than rewriting it. Keep `DocumentReconstituter` as the single shared full-stored-document assembler used by `RelationalWriteCurrentStateLoader` and external reads, and limit this task to residual deltas: canonical compiled JSON-path property order, any attachment fixes exposed by batched descriptor results, null handling, and the accepted empty-array behavior. Acceptance: reconstitution remains JsonNode-based for this story; deterministic object-member ordering follows canonical compiled JSON-path order; nested collections and scope-aligned extension rows continue attaching by stable scope identity after hydration-batch changes; `_ext` is omitted when a scope has no extension values; optional empty arrays are omitted; do not broaden this task into a wholesale rewrite of already-covered scalar/reference/descriptor/extension cases.
steps-to-verify: Add/extend focused `DocumentReconstituter` unit tests for deterministic property order, attachment behavior after batched descriptor changes, null handling, and omitted empty optional arrays.

description: Add a thin external-read materializer above `DocumentReconstituter`. Build a read-response layer that takes the fully reconstituted stored document plus `DocumentMetadataRow` and injects API metadata: `id` from `DocumentUuid`, `_etag` as the string `"{ContentVersion}"`, and `_lastModifiedDate` from `ContentLastModifiedAt` formatted in UTC second precision (`yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ`). Drive this from the local read mode so stored-document reads do not inject API-only fields. Acceptance: metadata comes from stored `dms.Document` stamps, not document hashing or `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow`; stored-document mode does not inject API-only fields; `ChangeVersion` remains deferred in DMS-990.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for metadata injection, UTC-second formatting, and separation between stored-document mode and external-response mode.

description: Finish readable-profile integration for relational GET-by-id and bypass legacy response filtering. Use `IReadableProfileProjector` only after full reconstitution and metadata injection in `ExternalResponse` mode, passing the minimal projection inputs from Core, and update `ReadableProfileProjector` to always preserve `id`, `_etag`, and `_lastModifiedDate` in addition to identity fields. Make `ProfileFilteringMiddleware` a no-op for relational GET-by-id responses so the old `ProfileResponseFilter` does not double-filter; keep non-relational reads on the current legacy path and leave query-specific readable projection behavior deferred with DMS-993 except for clean 501 stub handling. Acceptance: relational GET-by-id applies readable projection exactly once; relational GET-by-id responses with readable profiles still emit the profiled `Content-Type`; unsupported relational query responses do not run the legacy response filter on their way to 501; non-relational reads keep current legacy filtering behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add/extend unit tests for `ReadableProfileProjector` metadata preservation, relational `ProfileFilteringMiddleware` bypass behavior, relational GET-by-id profile `Content-Type` handling, and backend profile-flow coverage using reconstituted documents.

description: Add the external relational read execution seam needed by `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.GetDocumentById`. Activate a repository-facing read executor such as `IDocumentHydrator` for relational backend requests, wire provider-specific registrations for PostgreSQL and SQL Server, and reuse or extract the existing `(DocumentUuid, QualifiedResource)` lookup path so external reads can resolve a single-document keyset before hydration. Keep this task scoped to read execution infrastructure: provider DI, dialect routing, UUID/resource discrimination reuse, and single-document hydration invocation. Do not materialize API responses here; shared reconstitution, metadata injection, and readable projection remain owned by the repository materialization task. Acceptance: non-session relational reads can execute compiled hydration plans against both providers through an explicit external read seam; repository orchestration can request a hydrated single-document page without owning connection or dialect setup; UUIDs that resolve to the wrong resource type are distinguishable from missing documents for later `GetFailureNotExists` handling.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests covering provider-specific external read executor registration and the single-document UUID/resource lookup path reused by relational GET-by-id.

description: Implement `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.GetDocumentById` for non-descriptor relational-table resources on top of the explicit external read seam. Consume the hydrated single-document page, batch-provided descriptor lookup state, shared reconstitution, external response materialization, and optional readable projection to return real relational GET-by-id responses. Keep this task focused on repository materialization and result mapping, not provider setup or SQL execution infrastructure. Acceptance: when `UseRelationalBackend=true`, non-descriptor GET-by-id is active and does not fall back to the legacy repository; `GetFailureNotExists` covers both missing UUIDs and UUIDs that resolve to a different resource type; descriptor GET returns the explicit 501/not-implemented result until DMS-994; repository code clearly documents that relational authorization is still pending, does not attempt a temporary legacy auth path, and does not invoke the legacy resource authorization handler during relational GET-by-id.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for successful GET-by-id, not-found behavior, wrong-resource => not-exists behavior, descriptor-resource guard rails, query remaining deferred, and the assertion that relational GET-by-id does not call the legacy resource authorization handler.

description: Add the first end-to-end relational GET-by-id integration proof on PostgreSQL for one nested-plus-`_ext` resource fixture. Extend an authoritative relational backend integration test to write a representative document, fetch it back through `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.GetDocumentById`, and assert semantic JSON equivalence with strict array ordering, plus `id`, `_etag`, and `_lastModifiedDate`. Acceptance: PostgreSQL has a real relational write-then-read proof for a nested-plus-`_ext` scenario; integration assertions use semantic JSON equivalence rather than brittle object-property-order equality.
steps-to-verify: Add one PostgreSQL integration test that performs write then relational GET-by-id for the representative fixture and asserts nested collections, `_ext`, and metadata.

description: Port the same nested-plus-`_ext` relational GET-by-id proof to SQL Server once the PostgreSQL smoke is green. Keep this task scoped to provider parity and assertion reuse, not new read-path behavior. Acceptance: SQL Server has the same write-then-read proof for the representative fixture, asserting semantic JSON equivalence with strict array ordering, plus `id`, `_etag`, and `_lastModifiedDate`.
steps-to-verify: Add one SQL Server integration test that performs write then relational GET-by-id for the same representative fixture and asserts nested collections, `_ext`, and metadata.
